verdict_reasoning: SRC (proto-oncogene tyrosine-protein kinase Src) is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase that localizes to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. Its kinase domain, SH2, and SH3 domains all face the cytoplasm. There is no extracellular domain and no portion exposed to the outer leaflet. It is not accessible from the extracellular face by any modality. pMHC is the only indirect 'surface' story, which is explicitly excluded per scope rules.
